name: Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Villaviciosa de Córdoba, Pantano Puentenuevo, Espiel – ruta circular
description: This difficult 40.4-mile mountain bike route near Villaviciosa de Córdoba offers a challenging ride with 3665 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Villaviciosa de Córdoba are situated within the Sierra Morena mountain range, offering a rugged and scenic backdrop for outdoor activities. The region features mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes, providing challenging routes for experienced riders. The area is characterized by extensive trail networks, rich biodiversity, and the prominent Puente Nuevo Reservoir, which integrates into many cycling routes. This natural environment makes it a destination for mountain bikers seeking varied terrain. Best mountain bike trails around Villaviciosa De Córdoba The most popular mountain bike trail is Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop, a difficult 40.4 miles (65.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 38 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain around the central reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Alcubilla Spring – Arrastraculos Trail loop from Córdoba, a moderate 27.9 miles (45.0 km) path. This route features varied terrain leading through natural springs and trails. Local mountain bikers also love the Arenales Stream – Las Conejeras loop from Córdoba, an 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail leading through stream areas and natural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Mountain biking around Villaviciosa de Córdoba is defined by the Sierra Morena mountains, the Puente Nuevo Reservoir, and extensive trail networks. The region offers routes for various ability levels, from easy paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Villaviciosa de Córdoba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villaviciosa de Córdoba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Villaviciosa de Córdoba, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are situated within the rugged and scenic Sierra Morena mountain range.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villaviciosa de Córdoba?
A: The terrain in Villaviciosa de Córdoba is predominantly mountainous, characterized by the Sierra Morena range. You can expect rugged paths with significant elevation changes, offering both challenging climbs and rewarding descents. The area also features extensive trail networks, often integrating natural features like the Puente Nuevo Reservoir and various streams.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Villaviciosa de Córdoba?
A: Yes, Villaviciosa de Córdoba offers several routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39550672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenales Stream – Las Conejeras loop from Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 8.9-mile (14.3 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 17 minutes to complete, leading through stream areas and natural landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986646190/puente-nuevo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 40.4-mile (65.0 km) trail with over 3,600 feet of elevation gain, typically taking around 5 hours 38 minutes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33878825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo Bejarano – Elephant Fountain loop from Cerro Muriano&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 56 miles (89.5 km) with significant elevation.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/624215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Popea Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4631343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elephant Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, and various natural springs such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcubilla Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The Puente Nuevo Reservoir itself offers expansive views and is a central natural feature integrated into many routes.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Villaviciosa de Córdoba?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in Villaviciosa de Córdoba are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986646190/puente-nuevo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27644745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcubilla Spring – Arrastraculos Trail loop from Córdoba&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villaviciosa de Córdoba?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for mountain biking in Villaviciosa de Córdoba, offering pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. While summer can be hot, early mornings or late afternoons can still be suitable. The region's low light pollution also makes it excellent for stargazing, adding a unique dimension to evening outdoor activities.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Villaviciosa de Córdoba family-friendly?
A: While many trails offer challenging terrain, there are easier routes suitable for families, particularly those with some biking experience. The tranquil environment and rich biodiversity make it a great place for families to explore nature together. Always check the difficulty rating of a specific route to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails?
A: The Sierra Morena region, including Villaviciosa de Córdoba, is generally welcoming to dogs on trails, especially given its focus on outdoor activities and slow tourism. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Villaviciosa de Córdoba?
A: The mountain biking experience in Villaviciosa de Córdoba is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 15 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural scenery of the Sierra Morena, and the extensive trail networks that cater to different skill levels. The prominent Puente Nuevo Reservoir is also a frequently mentioned highlight.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can find historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4951467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino del Molinillo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4646005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of the Seven Corners&lt;/a&gt; are notable historical points of interest. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/632043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medina Azahara Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical attraction in the broader region.

Q: Where can I find amenities like cafes or accommodation in Villaviciosa de Córdoba?
A: Villaviciosa de Córdoba, as a 'slow tourism' destination, offers a tranquil environment with local amenities. You'll find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the town itself, providing convenient stops for refreshments or overnight stays after your rides. The low population density contributes to a relaxing experience.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is a challenging mountain bike route. It's classified as 'difficult' and requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills due to its length (approximately 65 km) and significant elevation changes (around 1,120 meters up and down).

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses a diverse landscape characteristic of the Sierra Morena. You'll encounter a mix of olive groves, dehesa (wooded pastures), pine forests, and riverside forests. The path includes dirt tracks, narrow trails, and may involve crossing streams like the Arroyo de las Carretas and Arroyo del Bejarano. Some segments wind through pine and eucalyptus forests and over firebreaks.

faqs.question: What natural features or wildlife might I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the rich natural heritage of the Sierra Morena. A key feature is the Puente Nuevo Reservoir. Keep an eye out for wildlife such as deer, wild boars, and fallow deer. Near water sources, you might also spot amphibians and various aquatic birds.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by the Puente Nuevo Reservoir, a significant natural feature. You'll also pass through areas like Cerro del Trigo – Mesas Altas and utilize parts of the Camino Espiel, offering a deep immersion in the natural environment.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending in Villaviciosa de Córdoba, it's recommended to park in or near the town. Specific parking areas aren't detailed, but public parking should be available within Villaviciosa de Córdoba, providing convenient access to the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Morena region can experience hot summers. Therefore, the best times to tackle this route are typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Winter can also be suitable, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Villaviciosa de Córdoba, Pantano Puentenuevo, Espiel – ruta circular dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route aren't provided, trails in natural areas like the Sierra Morena generally allow dogs. However, it's always best practice to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Be sure to carry water for your dog, especially on a long and challenging ride like this.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierra Morena park?
faqs.answer: This route primarily uses public paths, cattle trails, and riverbeds within the Sierra Morena. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for accessing these types of trails in the Sierra Morena. However, always respect private property and any local signage you may encounter.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Mozárabe (approximately 12% of the route), the Sendero de Sierra Morena (about 11%), and a small portion of the SL-A 75 (around 1%).

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging and remote nature of this 65 km route, amenities like cafes or dedicated water refill stations are likely scarce once you leave Villaviciosa de Córdoba. It's crucial to carry ample water and snacks for the entire duration of your ride. While there are stream crossings and reservoirs, it's not advisable to rely on untreated natural water sources for drinking.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: For this difficult mountain bike route, wear appropriate cycling gear, including a helmet, padded shorts, and layers suitable for the weather. Bring plenty of water (at least 2-3 liters), energy-rich snacks, a basic bike repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, sun protection, and a fully charged phone with the komoot app for navigation. Given the potential for less defined paths, GPS navigation is highly recommended.
